title: Terniola ceylanica
taxonomy: Terniola ceylanica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Terniola and is native to Sri Lanka. The classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower structure. Terniola ceylanica is further classified into several infraspecific taxa, including varieties and subspecies. This classification helps in understanding the plant's evolutionary relationships and its position within the plant kingdom.
uses: Terniola ceylanica has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help in treating various health conditions. The plant is also used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Its leaves and roots are used to make medicinal teas and infusions. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Terniola ceylanica for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Terniola ceylanica is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is believed to have several health benefits. The plant's flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities.
